solution.p: Since we are not allowed to divide by zero, the denominator of \displaystyle{K} cannot be zero. To find what x values will make the denominator zero, we solve the equation:
We need to take -10 and 10 out of the functions domain, because they will cause the denominator to be zero, and we cannot divide by zero. The domain of K is:
We could also use set notation to represent the domain:
